elements.sent-2411-001: We describe the canonical module of a simplicial affine semigroup ring $\KK[S]$ and its trace ideal.
elements.sent-2411-002: As a consequence, we characterize when $\KK[S]$ is nearly Gorenstein in terms of arithmetic properties of the semigroup $S$.
elements.sent-2411-003: Then, we find some bounds for the Cohen-Macaulay type of $\KK[S]$ when it is nearly Gorenstein.
elements.sent-2411-004: In particular, if it has codimension at most three, we prove that the Cohen-Macaulay type is at most three and this bound is sharp.
elements.sent-2411-005: The notion of nearly Gorenstein ring appeared several times in literature as a generalization of Gorenstein rings, even if this name was introduced later in \cite{HHS , see for instance \cite{D,HV,SV .}}
elements.sent-2411-006: Indeed, only in 2019, with the work of Herzog, Hibi, and Stamate \cite{HHS , a systematic study of these rings has begun.}

elements.sent-2411-008: The idea behind nearly Gorenstein rings relies on the fact that the trace ideal of the canonical module of a ring determines its non-Gorenstein locus.
elements.sent-2411-009: More precisely, let $(R,\mathfrak m)$ be a Cohen-Macaulay local ring (or a positively graded $\KK$-algebra) admitting canonical module $\omega_R$, and define the trace ideal of $\omega_R$ as the ideal \begin{equation}\tr(\omega_R)= \sum_{\varphi \in \Hom_R(\omega_R,R)} \varphi(\omega_R).\end{equation}.
elements.sent-2411-010: Given a prime ideal $\mathfrak{p \in {\rm Spec (R)$, the ring $R_{\mathfrak{p }$ is not Gorenstein if and only if $\tr(\omega_R) \subseteq \mathfrak{p}$, see \cite[Lemma 2.1]{HHS .}}}}
elements.sent-2411-011: Therefore, $R$ is Gorenstein if and only if $\tr(\omega_R)=R$.
elements.sent-2411-012: The ring $R$ is said to be nearly Gorenstein if $\tr(\omega_R)$ contains the maximal (homogeneous) ideal $\mathfrak{m}$ of $R$, i.e., if $\tr(\omega_R)$ is equal to either $R$ or $\mathfrak{m}$.
elements.sent-2411-013: It is clear that a nearly Gorenstein ring is Gorenstein in the punctured spectrum, but the converse is not true; indeed, $R$ is Gorenstein on the punctured spectrum if and only if $\tr(\omega_R)$ is an $\mathfrak{m}$-primary ideal.
elements.sent-2411-014: In this paper we are interested in the nearly Gorenstein affine semigroup rings, and especially in their Cohen-Macaulay type, which we call only type for brevity.
